name: Fontana
metaTitle: Fontana Accident Guide — Local Hospitals, Courts & Rights
metaDescription: Injured in Fontana? Find local emergency hospitals, courts, and understand your rights under California personal injury law.
description: Fontana is a major Inland Empire city in San Bernardino County that has become one of Southern California's most significant warehouse and logistics hubs. The I-10 and I-15 freeways intersect near Fontana's core, and the resulting interchange is one of the highest-volume commercial truck collision zones in the region. Hundreds of distribution centers and fulfillment warehouses operate near these corridors, generating relentless heavy vehicle traffic across Sierra Ave, Foothill Blvd, and Arrow Blvd throughout all hours of the day. SR-210 runs along the northern edge of the city, providing commuter connectivity toward San Bernardino and Pasadena. Baseline Road connects Fontana to neighboring Rancho Cucamonga and Ontario, carrying both commercial and residential traffic. The concentration of logistics employers also means a higher-than-average proportion of commercial vehicle drivers operating under time pressure, increasing the risk of fatigued or distracted driving accidents. Omnitrans bus routes serve the city extensively, and transit-related injuries may name Omnitrans as a defendant. Kaiser Permanente Fontana serves as the primary ER for much of the western San Bernardino Valley.

courts.name: San Bernardino Superior Court — Fontana District

commonAccidentTypes: Commercial truck accidents on I-10 near Inland Empire distribution center access routes
High-speed interchange collisions at the I-10 and I-15 junction
Lane-change and rear-end accidents on SR-210 during peak commute hours
Pedestrian and cyclist accidents on Sierra Ave and Foothill Blvd near logistics campuses
Intersection collisions on Arrow Blvd near warehouse district driveways
Fatigued and distracted driver accidents on Baseline Rd from logistics shift workers
notableCorridors: I-10 (San Bernardino Freeway) — major freight corridor with constant commercial truck volume
I-15 (Devore Freeway) — north-south freeway connecting to Las Vegas corridor and San Diego
SR-210 (Foothill Freeway) — commuter freeway along the northern city edge
Sierra Ave — primary north-south arterial adjacent to warehouse and distribution districts
Foothill Blvd — historic Route 66 corridor with mixed commercial and residential traffic
Arrow Blvd — logistics district access road with heavy driveway conflict and truck turns
localNotes: Fontana is in San Bernardino County. Accidents involving city or county property, sidewalks, or government vehicles require a government claim filed within 6 months of the injury date. Fontana Police Department traffic collision reports can be requested through FPD Records. Omnitrans bus service is a potential defendant in transit-related injury cases. Large logistics companies operating in Fontana may also be defendants if their drivers caused the accident.
